title: Lam Research (LRCX) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 87/100 — Strong Prospect.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 4.0% outpaces global GDP growth. The semiconductor capital equipment sector is entering a "supercycle" where baseline semiconductor content in cars, data centers, and consumer electronics mandates higher perpetual growth.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: Why the Supercycle is Real
thesisParagraphs: Lam Research is a critical supplier of wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) for semiconductor manufacturing. They hold an essential near-monopoly on certain etch and deposition tools required to make advanced memory (NAND/DRAM) and logic chips. As AI drives insatiable demand for HBM memory and smaller node architectures, LRCX's specialized tools are seeing sustained high demand, bypassing typical cyclic downturns.

My valuation model assumes that the massive CapEx investments by leading foundries and memory makers are structural. This shifts Lam Research from a purely cyclical stock into a secular growth story, driving robust margin expansion and unparalleled Free Cash Flow (FCF) generation over the next five years.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 16% growth rate for Lam Research?
faq.answer: Gemini projects that as AI drives demand for more advanced chips and HBM memory, LRCX's etch and deposition tools will see sustained high demand, leading to strong margin expansion and FCF growth.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for Lam Research's DCF?
faq.answer: A 10.5% discount rate was selected. This reflects a slightly higher beta given the cyclicality of semis, combined with a 4.18% risk-free rate, plus equity risk premium, offset by LRCX's strong balance sheet.

faq.question: Is Lam Research cyclical?
faq.answer: Yes. Lam Research operates in the highly cyclical semiconductor capital equipment market, which means cash flow naturally ebbs and flows with the broader semi cycle. Valuations must account for these historical drawdowns.

prospectVerdictDetail: Lam Research maintains a robust position in the semiconductor equipment market, particularly in front-end wafer processing. Its critical role in enabling advanced transistor geometries creates immense switching costs for fabs. The company is poised to benefit significantly from the ongoing global buildout of fab capacity and the increasing capital intensity of manufacturing next-generation chips.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Assessment of the company's competitive momentum, market share trajectory, and product velocity.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs Peers
factors.rationale: Lam's growth outpaces traditional industrials, driven by its exposure to the secular growth of the semiconductor sector and increasing capital intensity.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company maintains a highly concentrated and dominant market share in crucial etch and deposition processes, particularly for memory chips.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: High pricing power stems from the mission-critical nature of its multi-million dollar tools, where performance and reliability far outweigh initial cost for foundries.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Continuous innovation is required to keep pace with Moore's Law and shrinking node geometries, ensuring a steady stream of new, high-margin equipment.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Evaluation of the durability of the company's economic moat, including switching costs, network effects, and capital intensity.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Extremely high switching costs define the moat. Fabs optimize entire manufacturing lines around specific toolsets; changing vendors requires massive re-qualification and unacceptable downtime.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The massive installed base of tools provides critical process data, creating a feedback loop that allows Lam to continually refine recipes and improve tool performance for all customers.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Holds a formidable patent portfolio in deposition and etch technologies. However, export controls targeting certain regions present an ongoing regulatory risk.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: While the end product is capital-intensive for the buyer, Lam's own manufacturing process is highly efficient, generating robust free cash flows.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Analysis of market sentiment, earnings estimate revisions, and management capital allocation.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts consistently revise estimates upward as visibility into fab expansions improves and the AI infrastructure buildout accelerates.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Sentiment is highly positive, driven by the narrative of semiconductors being the new oil, placing equipment providers in a highly strategic and favored position.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has a strong track record of shareholder returns through substantial buybacks and a growing dividend, alongside prudent R&D investments.

prospectKeyRisks: Cyclical downturns in memory chip capital expenditures.
Geopolitical tensions affecting sales in the Chinese market.
Intense competition from other major wafer fabrication equipment manufacturers.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Increased adoption of advanced packaging and complex 3D NAND architectures.
Significant global government subsidies driving new domestic fab construction.
Expansion of AI-driven computing demanding more advanced and capital-intensive semiconductor manufacturing.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored LRCX at 85/100 and Opus at 88/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
